name: io.github.ashlrai/phantom-secrets-mcp
title: Phantom Secrets
description: MCP server that prevents AI coding agents from leaking API keys. Secrets are stored in an OS keychain vault and replaced with opaque phantom tokens so agents never see real values.

tools.name: phantom_list_secrets
tools.description: List all secret names in the Phantom vault. Returns names only — never exposes actual secret values. Use this to see what secrets are configured.

tools.name: phantom_status
tools.description: Show Phantom status: project ID, vault backend, number of secrets, configured services, and proxy state.

tools.name: phantom_init
tools.description: Initialize Phantom: read .env file, store real secrets in the vault, and rewrite .env with phantom tokens. Mutating; requires confirm: true.

env_path.description: Path to the .env file (defaults to .env in current directory)

confirm.description: Must be true — init stores secrets and rewrites .env.

tools.name: phantom_add_secret
tools.description: Deprecated unsafe plaintext secret entry. This tool refuses values because MCP arguments enter agent context. Use phantom_add_secret_interactive instead.

value.description: Deprecated; plaintext values are rejected through MCP

confirm.description: Must be true — the calling agent must confirm with the user before invoking this tool.

tools.name: phantom_add_secret_interactive
tools.description: Safely add a secret by name without passing its value through MCP. Requires confirm:true. The returned command prompts for the value directly in the user's terminal, outside agent context.

name.description: Name of the secret to add

confirm.description: Must be true — the calling agent must confirm with the user before invoking this tool.

tools.name: phantom_remove_secret
tools.description: Remove a secret from the Phantom vault by name. DESTRUCTIVE — the secret is permanently deleted. Requires confirm: true; the agent must ask the user for explicit consent before calling.

name.description: Name of the secret to remove

confirm.description: Must be true — the calling agent must confirm with the user before invoking this tool.

tools.name: phantom_rotate
tools.description: Regenerate all phantom tokens in .env. Old tokens become invalid — any running phantom exec or dev server will break until it picks up the new .env. Real secrets in the vault are unchanged. DESTRUCTIVE; requires confirm: true.

confirm.description: Must be true — the calling agent must confirm with the user before invoking this tool.

tools.name: phantom_cloud_push
tools.description: Push local vault to Phantom Cloud. Encrypts secrets client-side before upload; server never sees plaintext. Requires phantom login first. DESTRUCTIVE — overwrites the existing cloud copy. Requires confirm: true; the agent must ask the user for explicit consent before calling.

confirm.description: Must be true — the calling agent must confirm with the user before invoking this tool.

tools.name: phantom_cloud_pull
tools.description: Pull vault from Phantom Cloud to local machine. Decrypts client-side. Use force=true to overwrite existing secrets. DESTRUCTIVE — writes entries into the local vault. Requires confirm: true; the agent must ask the user for explicit consent before calling.

confirm.description: Must be true — the calling agent must confirm with the user before invoking this tool.

tools.name: phantom_cloud_status
tools.description: Check Phantom Cloud authentication status, plan, and last sync version.

tools.name: phantom_copy_secret
tools.description: Copy a secret from this project's vault to another project's vault. The secret value is never exposed — it transfers directly between encrypted vaults. DESTRUCTIVE — writes a secret into another vault; requires confirm: true; the agent must ask the user for explicit consent before calling.

name.description: Name of the secret to copy from the current project

target_dir.description: Path to the target project directory (must be phantom-initialized)

rename.description: Optional new name for the secret in the target project

confirm.description: Must be true — the calling agent must confirm with the user before invoking this tool.

tools.name: phantom_doctor
tools.description: Run Phantom health checks: config validity, vault access, .env protection, .gitignore, .env.example, pre-commit hook. Set fix=true to auto-fix safe issues.

fix.description: Auto-fix safe issues (install hooks, generate .env.example, etc.)

tools.name: phantom_why
tools.description: Explain why an environment variable is or isn't protected by Phantom. Shows classification (Secret, PublicKey, NotSecret), whether it has a phantom token, and what heuristic matched.

tools.name: phantom_wrap
tools.description: Wrap package.json scripts with npx phantom-secrets exec so secrets are injected via the proxy at runtime. Saves originals as script:raw variants. Uses a heuristic to pick dev/start/build/serve/deploy scripts and skip lint/test/format scripts.

only.description: Only wrap specific scripts (by name). If empty, uses default heuristic.

tools.name: phantom_unwrap
tools.description: Reverse phantom_wrap: restore original package.json scripts from their :raw variants and remove the :raw entries.

tools.name: phantom_check
tools.description: Check for security issues. With runtime=true, scans current environment for phantom tokens without a proxy (leak detection). Otherwise, scans .env files for unprotected real secrets.

runtime.description: Check if phantom tokens are in environment without proxy running

tools.name: phantom_env
tools.description: Generate a .env.example file from .env. Secrets are replaced with descriptive placeholders; non-secret config values are preserved. Safe to commit to version control.

tools.name: phantom_sync
tools.description: Show sync configuration and what secrets would be synced to deployment platforms (Vercel, Railway). This is an informational tool — actual sync requires platform API tokens. Use it to understand and explain the sync setup.

platform.description: Platform to sync to (vercel, railway). If empty, shows all configured targets.

project_id.description: Override project ID for this sync
